hero.name: Yingge Hu / Craig
hero.title: Computer Science Researcher & Data Engineer
hero.summary: Specializing in Database Architecture, Disaggregated Database, Data Management，Machine Learning. Passionate about bridging theoretical concepts with high-performance computing to solve complex data challenges.

news.title: Recent Updates
news.subtitle: A timeline of my academic and professional journey

items.text: Started new research on Differential Privacy under the guidance of Professor Mostafa Milani at Western University's DB Lab.
Began Master of Science in Computer Science at Western University.
Graduated from University of Toronto in Electrical and Computer Engineering.
Received the Iron Ring for Canadian Engineering.
Successfully completed extensive research projects on Cardiac MRI Denoising (using Restormer and Swin-UNet) and Brain MRI Tumor Classification.
Became Web Director for University of Toronto Robotics Association (UTRA).
Started new research on Network Bottleneck for Disaggregated Database systems under the guidance of Professor Qizhen Zhang at University of Toronto Far Data Lab.
Started Data Engineer intern at Coca Cola in the O2O team.
Volunteered to build a painting community for FF14 and updated weekly events.

schools.name: University of Toronto
schools.degree: Bachelor of Applied Science in Computer Engineering
schools.courses: Focus: Disaggregated Database, Hyperscale Data Processing, Machine Learning

items.desc: Conducting research on Differential Privacy under the guidance of Professor Mostafa Milani, investigating privacy-preserving mechanisms for data management systems.

items.org: University of Toronto Far Data Lab
items.desc: Collaborated with Professor Qizhen Zhang on state-of-the-art Hyperscale Data Management, focusing on disaggregated database systems and network performance analysis.
bullets: Built an eBPF-based latency tracing tool for TCP/IP/MAC layers to analyze cross-layer overhead in TiDB transactions.
Deployed and evaluated TiDB on CloudLab in disaggregated data settings, studying the impact of bandwidth and storage placement on distributed transaction performance.
Investigated TiDB transaction internals through source code and papers, and instrumented the codebase to measure latency across critical execution paths.
Improved TPC-C benchmark performance through configuration tuning source code guided by Prometheus and dashboard metrics, including experiments with RAMDisk-backed storage.

items.desc: Developed storage procedures and built pipelines to monitor sales performance using Databricks on Azure. Conducted performance and user behavior analysis with Power BI, and optimized data transfer methods on Azure Blob Storage.

items.name: Research Assistent
items.desc: Research in Differential Privacy related to pufferfish and blowfish framework.

items.name: Research Assistent
items.desc: Built an eBPF-based latency tracing tool for TCP/IP/MAC layers to analyze cross-layer overhead in TiDB transactions. Deployed and evaluated TiDB on CloudLab in disaggregated data settings, studying the impact of bandwidth and storage placement on distributed transaction performance. Investigated TiDB transaction internals through source code and papers, and instrumented the codebase to measure latency across critical execution paths. Improved TPC-C benchmark performance through configuration tuning source code guided by Prometheus and dashboard metrics, including experiments with RAMDisk-backed storage.

items.name: Cardiac MRI Denoising & Brain Tumor Classification
items.tech: Python, PyTorch, Restormer, Swin-UNet, MaxViT, Grad-CAM++
items.desc: Developed a denoising pipeline for cardiac MRIs using proxy clean/noisy image pairs (simulating mixed Gaussian and Rician noise) to overcome the lack of paired clinical data. Outperformed classical BM3D algorithms in PSNR and SSIM. Further evaluated the proxy-trained Restormer's effect on downstream 4-class brain tumor classification using MaxViT, utilizing Grad-CAM++ and quantitative attention metrics to analyze spatial evidence usage.

items.name: Sleep Detection with Smart Wristbands
items.desc: Developed a sleep detection model utilizing accelerometer data, implementing an RCNN architecture combining CNN and RNN for time-series analysis.

items.name: Map for Tourists: GIS Application
items.desc: Built a Geographical Information System featuring path highlighting and the A* algorithm for shortest-path solutions.

items.name: University of Toronto Robotics Association
items.desc: I volunteered as a web director for UTRA website in 2024-2025.

items.name: Game Community Supporter
items.desc: I volunteered design and update activities for game community, preserved as an archive to showcase past web development work.
